title: wollastonia biflora var. ryukyuensis
intro: Wollastonia biflora var. ryukyuensis, also known as Ryukyu wollastonia, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Ryukyu Islands in Japan. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to possess health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. In traditional medicine, Ryukyu wollastonia is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves, stems, and roots are used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments.
